# DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md — spring-mvc-demo
*Generated by Layer 0 prompt. Reviewed and corrected manually.*

## Coding Conventions
- Controllers delegate immediately — no logic beyond validation and service call
- Services own all business logic and in-memory state
- All public methods have Javadoc — one sentence minimum
- Constructor injection only — no `@Autowired` on fields
- Records for all model and DTO types — no mutable POJOs

## Naming Patterns
| Element | Pattern | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Controllers | `{Resource}Controller` | `ProductController` |
| Services | `{Resource}Service` | `ProductService` |
| Domain records | `{Entity}` | `Product` |
| Request records | `Create{Resource}Request` | `CreateProductRequest` |
| Exceptions | `{Resource}NotFoundException` | `ProductNotFoundException` |
| Error response | `ErrorResponse` | `ErrorResponse` |
| Tests | `{Class}Test` | `ProductServiceTest` |
| Test methods | `method_condition_expectedResult` | `getById_unknownId_throwsProductNotFoundException` |

## State Management
All state lives in `ProductService.store` — a `ConcurrentHashMap<UUID, Product>`.
Service methods are otherwise stateless. No state in controllers, exceptions, or handlers.

## Error Handling
- Named exception per failure category — `ProductNotFoundException` for missing products
- Exceptions thrown at the service boundary where the failure is detected
- `GlobalExceptionHandler` maps exceptions to HTTP — mapping never lives in service code
- Never throw bare `RuntimeException` — always a named subclass

## Testing Approach
- Service layer: plain JUnit 5, no Spring context, `new ProductService()` in `@BeforeEach`
- Controller layer: `@WebMvcTest`, `@MockBean ProductService`, `MockMvc` for HTTP assertions
- Assert on `$.code` in error response tests — never on `$.message` text
- What is tested: happy path per endpoint, not-found per ID endpoint, validation failures

## Design Constraints
- Do not use `@Autowired` on fields — constructor injection only
- Do not add repository interfaces — service owns the map directly
- Do not use `@SpringBootTest` — too broad; use `@WebMvcTest` for controllers, plain JUnit for services
- Do not assert on `$.message` in tests — assert on `$.code` only
- Do not create service interfaces — `@Service` class directly, no interface unless multiple implementations exist
